citations.description: config_manual block: fire-and-forget err call with no state file, no re-emit, no acknowledgment mechanism
Ticket comment (RebarBotNava): 'AC2 (durable config_manual pending-marker in autodeploy.sh) remains — that is the code portion of this ticket.'

findings.criterion: config_manual signals persist (re-emitted or alarmed) until acknowledged.
findings.detail: The fire-and-forget behavior is not fixed in infra/scripts/autodeploy.sh. Lines 173-177 show the config_manual block still calls `err config_manual ...` once and continues with no state file written, no pending-manual marker, and no re-emission logic. The bug (a single signal that is silently swallowed after deployed-sha advances) remains in the code. The ticket comment acknowledges AC2 is outstanding ('AC2 (durable config_manual pending-marker in autodeploy.sh) remains') and a subsequent comment notes it was 'superseded by epic 992d-9d17-c6ac-4492', but this bug ticket remains open and the codebase-verifiable criterion is not satisfied.
findings.remediation: Implement a state file (e.g. $STATE_DIR/pending-config-manual) written when config_manual is detected, cleared only when an operator explicitly acknowledges it, and re-emit the AUTODEPLOY_ERROR on every run while the file is present. Or close this ticket explicitly in favor of epic 992d-9d17-c6ac-4492 story a0d0 once that epic's fix lands.
findings.title: Durable pending-manual-apply marker not implemented in autodeploy.sh

data.remediation: How to resolve the unmet criteria: for each one, decide whether the work is genuinely incomplete or simply undocumented. If a requirement is already satisfied but the proof is not discoverable from the code alone, add a comment to this ticket that documents the evidence — cite the concrete artifacts that meet the requirement (file paths and line ranges, commands and their output, links, or the reasoning that ties the work to the criterion). The completion verifier reads this ticket's comments, so evidence you record there is taken into account on the next verification. For any criterion whose work is genuinely unfinished, complete it, then re-verify.
